sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: The animation changed a lot (117 cells, bbox [8,14] to [43,42]). This suggests the player moved significantly, possibly to the red path area and started moving down. Let me check the player's new position.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check new player position
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']
blue_player = [n for n in nodes if n['color'] == 'b' and n['pixels'] == 24]
print(f"Player position: {blue_player[0]['boundary']}")

# Check the red path - did it change?
red_nodes = [n for n in nodes if n['color'] == 'R']
print(f"Red path nodes: {len(red_nodes)}")
for n in red_nodes:
    print(f"  pixels={n['pixels']}, boundary={n['boundary'][:3]}...")

# Check area around player
ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
p = blue_player[0]['boundary']
r_min, r_max = p[0][0], p[2][0]
c_min, c_max = p[0][1], p[2][1]
print(f"\nPlayer area (rows {r_min-2}-{r_max+2}, cols {c_min-2}-{c_max+6}):")
for i in range(max(0, r_min-2), min(63, r_max+3)):
    print(f"{i:2d}: {ascii_lines[i][max(0,c_min-2):min(64,c_max+6)]}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
